title: Warm, Comforting Peruvian Pea-Eggplant Chupe
description: A hearty, comforting Peruvian dish featuring tender pea-eggplant in a velvety, winey sauce, perfect for a chilly evening. This lacto-vegetarian-friendly recipe is easy to make and serves 4-6 people.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add 1 diced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add 3 minced garlic cloves and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add 1 1/2 pounds peas and eggplant, diced, and cook until the vegetables start to soften, about 5 minutes.
Add 1 cup red wine and 1 cup chicken broth, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover the saucepan, and simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in 1 teaspoon ground cumin, 1 teaspoon paprika, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon black pepper.
If using heavy cream, stir it in at this point.
Add 1-2 teaspoons cayenne pepper, depending on your desired level of heat.
Taste and adjust seasonings as needed.
If desired, stir in 1/4 cup chopped fresh cilantro and 1-2 tablespoons crushed candy.
Serve hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
